codeReview.summary: Fail. The change and new tests only cover a mocked path where `ctx.prompt()` throws, but the real missing-provider runtime from VAL-LIVE-010 still reports the later generic `onError('Agent not initialized')` and returns. Because `AgentBridge` still overwrites `scoopError` on every callback, the specific init-time message is not preserved on the actual `ScoopContext` path. The `finally` cleanup block is unchanged, so I did not find a new cleanup regression.

issues.description: The motivating bug is still present on the real `ScoopContext` path. `AgentBridge` still overwrites `scoopError` on every `onError` callback (`agent-bridge.ts:304`), and after `await ctx.prompt(...)` it still promotes the last callback to `finalText` (`agent-bridge.ts:352-354`). But `ScoopContext.init()` surfaces the specific missing-provider error via `callbacks.onError('Failed to initialize: ...')` and returns (`scoop-context.ts:301`), then `ScoopContext.prompt()` hits `if (!this.agent) { this.callbacks.onError('Agent not initialized'); return; }` (`scoop-context.ts:307-308`). So the later generic callback still replaces the specific init error before the bridge reaches the new catch branch (`agent-bridge.ts:368`). This matches the live evidence in `val-live-010.json:165-166,213`, which already identified that the LAST `scoopError` wins. The added test only covers a mocked `prompt()` throw (`agent-bridge.test.ts:1566-1592`), so it does not exercise the actual runtime behavior that originally failed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state for this follow-up fix points workers at the wrong root cause. `features.json` describes the bug as a catch-branch problem where `ctx.prompt()` throws and instructs workers to implement `finalText = scoopError ?? errText(err)`, but the recorded live evidence had already concluded that the real issue was the bridge keeping the LAST `onError` (`'Agent not initialized'`) instead of the earlier specific init error.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/trieloff/Developer/slicc/.yolo/droid-2/.factory/validation/hardening/user-testing/flows/val-live-010.json:165-166,213` says the LAST `scoopError` wins and suggests capturing the first callback or ignoring the later generic one; `/Users/trieloff/.factory/missions/094c0314-6896-4f76-bf58-89b7dfee66f4/features.json:1682` prescribes the catch-branch fix and a mocked throwing-`prompt()` test instead. That mismatch appears to have driven this incorrect implementation.

summary: Fail. This follow-up fix does not look correct: it patches only the mocked throw path, while the real missing-provider flow from VAL-LIVE-010 still collapses to `Agent not initialized`. I did not see a new cleanup regression, but the blocking output bug remains unresolved.
